title.en: Invited to a Korean home? Gift Etiquette & Phrases
title.ko: 뭐 이런 걸 다 가져왔어요! (초대와 선물 리액션)

description.en: Don't go empty-handed! Learn the art of "Jeong" (Korean affection) and what to say when giving or receiving gifts at a friend's home.
description.ko: 친구 집에 초대받았을 때! 빈손으로 가기 미안한 마음을 전하는 법과 선물을 주고받을 때 나누는 따뜻한 한국식 리액션을 배워봅니다.

tags: Situation. Social. Gift. Etiquette. Home Visit. Jeong. Season 2

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina, I’m visiting a Korean friend’s house for the first time today. I bought some fruit, but I’m worried about what to say when I hand it over. In English, we just say "This is for you," but is there a more "Korean" way?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오! 좋은 질문이에요! 한국에서는 선물을 줄 때 정성 이라는 마음을 표현하는 게 중요해요.
content.translation: Oh, good question! In Korea, it's important to express the feeling that "it's my sincerity" when giving a gift.

content.speaker: John
content.text: And I heard that when Koreans receive a gift, they sometimes say 뭐 이런 걸 다 가져왔어요? It sounds like they are asking "Why did you bring this?"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 절대 기분 나쁜 뜻이 아니에요! "이렇게까지 안 챙겨줘도 되는데 고맙다" 라는 아주 정중한 리액션이죠.
content.translation: Haha, it's definitely not a bad thing! It's a very polite reaction meaning "You didn't have to go this far, but thank you."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오늘은 선물을 주고받을 때 쓰는 리얼한 표현들을 배워볼게요!
content.translation: Today, we'll learn real expressions for giving and receiving gifts!

content.speaker: John
content.text: '뭐 이런 걸 다 가져왔어요.' So it's a polite way of saying "You shouldn't have" right?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 상대방의 수고에 고마움을 표현하는 전형적인 한국식 리액션이에요.
content.translation: Right! It's a typical Korean reaction expressing gratitude for the other person's effort.

insight.title: 뭐 이런 걸 다 가져왔어요
insight.usage_tip: '뭐(What)' + '이런 걸(Something like this)' + '다(All)' + '가져왔어요(Brought)'. It is a polite refusal of the burden, not the gift itself.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 뭐 이런 걸 다 가져왔어요!
content.translation: Oh, Mina! You shouldn't have brought all this!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Then what should I say when I give the gift?

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "오다가 생각나서 좀 샀어요" 라고 해보세요. "큰 건 아니지만 네 생각이 나서 준비했다" 라는 아주 친근하고 겸손한 표현이에요.
content.translation: Try saying "오다가 생각나서 좀 샀어요". It's a very friendly and humble expression meaning "It's not much, but I prepared it because I thought of you."

insight.title: 오다가 생각나서 좀 샀어요
insight.description: 'I picked this up on my way here'
insight.usage_tip: '오다가(On the way)' + '생각나서(Because I thought of you)' + '좀 샀어요(Bought a bit)'. Koreans use this to lower the pressure of the gift.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Okay, let's jump in. I'm arriving at Mina's house for a dinner invitation. I have a box of delicious strawberries in my hand.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 존! 어서 와요. 집 찾기 힘들지 않았어요?
content.translation: Oh, John! Welcome. Was it hard to find the house?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 아니요. 금방 찾았어요. 여기요. 오다가 생각나서 좀 샀어요.
content.translation: No, I found it quickly. Here, I picked this up on my way here.

insight.title: 오다가 생각나서 좀 샀어요
insight.usage_tip: Use this even if you spent a long time choosing the gift to show modesty.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 우와! 딸기네요! 뭐 이런 걸 다 가져왔어요! 그냥 와도 되는데.
content.translation: Wow, strawberries! John, you shouldn't have brought all this! You could have just come.

insight.title: 그냥 와도 되는데
insight.usage_tip: A classic set phrase used with '뭐 이런 걸 다...' to show they didn't expect a gift.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 부담 갖지 마세요. 미나씨가 딸기 좋아한다고 해서 준비했어요.
content.translation: Please don't feel burdened. I prepared it because I heard you like strawberries.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말 고마워요. 우리 같이 맛있게 먹어요!
content.translation: Thank you so much. Let's eat these together!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I love how these phrases make the exchange feel so warm and less like a formal transaction.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 한국의 선물 문화는 물건보다 그 속에 담긴 마음을 주고받는 리액션이 핵심이거든요.
content.translation: Right. In Korean gift culture, the core is the reaction of exchanging the 'heart' inside the object rather than the object itself.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review our "gift-related" reactions one last time.

content.speaker: John
content.text: 뭐 이런 걸 다 가져왔어요!
content.translation: You shouldn't have brought all this!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 오다가 생각나서 좀 샀어요!
content.translation: I picked this up on my way here!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 작은 성의예요. 부담 갖지 마세요!
content.translation: It's a small token of my sincerity. Don't feel burdened!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 그냥 오셔도 되는데!

content.speaker: John
content.text: 잘 먹을게요! 잘 쓸게요!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 누군가의 집을 방문한다면 소중한 마음을 담은 리액션을 건네보세요.
content.translation: Everyone, if you visit someone's home today, try offering a reaction filled with your precious heart. "I picked this up on my way here!"

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 친구 집에 빈손으로 가기 미안해서 음료수를 샀어요. (I bought some drinks because I felt bad going to my friend's house empty-handed.)
선물의 가격보다 정성이 담긴 마음이 더 중요해요. (The sincere heart is more important than the price of the gift.)
비싼 선물은 받는 사람에게 부담을 줄 수 있어요. (Expensive gifts can give a burden to the receiver.)
그 대답은 한국 사람들의 전형적인 인사치레예요. (That answer is a typical Korean courtesy.)
(Note: Reused for review) 안색이 좋아 보여서 다행이네요. (I'm glad your complexion looks good.)
작은 성의지만 제 진심을 담아 준비했어요. (It's a small token of sincerity, but I prepared it with my heart.)
